title: Ultra HNW Client Documentation Standards
content:
TO: Private Wealth Management Team
FROM: Compliance Department
RE: Enhanced Documentation Standards for Ultra High Net Worth Clients

Effective immediately, all Private Wealth advisors must implement enhanced documentation protocols for Ultra HNW clients with investable assets exceeding $25 million. These standards ensure full compliance with FINRA Rule 4512 customer account information requirements and SEC Regulation S-P privacy protections.

CLIENT IDENTIFICATION REQUIREMENTS:
Complete Know Your Customer documentation must include verified government-issued identification, proof of address within 90 days, and beneficial ownership disclosure for all entities. For example, our recent onboarding of Robert J. Williams required additional verification due to his complex offshore trust structures totaling $187 million in managed assets. All supporting documentation must be maintained in our secure vault system with restricted access protocols.

COMMUNICATION STANDARDS:
All client communications exceeding $1 million in transaction value require written confirmation within 24 hours. Phone conversations must be documented with detailed notes, including callback verification to registered numbers. When we processed Mr. Williams' $45 million private equity allocation last quarter, we confirmed instructions via his registered contact number (212) 555-0147 and maintained recorded verification per our enhanced protocols.

ACCOUNT MONITORING:
Ultra HNW accounts require monthly comprehensive reviews regardless of activity level. Investment policy statements must be updated annually or following any material change in financial circumstances exceeding 15% of net worth. Account statements for portfolios above $50 million are delivered via secure courier service with signature confirmation. Recent portfolio restructuring for Account #4821-7734 demonstrates our rigorous approval process, requiring Managing Director sign-off for the $78 million asset reallocation.

PRIVACY PROTECTION:
SEC Regulation S-P mandates specific safeguards for nonpublic personal information. Ultra HNW client data requires encryption at rest and in transit, with access limited to relationship team members plus designated compliance personnel. Client names cannot appear in general correspondence or meeting notes accessible to support staff. Physical documents must remain in locked filing systems with dual-key access requirements.

REPORTING OBLIGATIONS:
Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) thresholds are reduced to $5,000 for cash transactions involving Ultra HNW clients due to enhanced scrutiny requirements. Large cash position reporting applies to liquid holdings exceeding $2.5 million maintained for periods longer than 30 days. Currency Transaction Reports must be filed for any cash equivalent movements above $10,000 within rolling 30-day periods.

COMPLIANCE MONITORING:
Quarterly compliance reviews now include specific Ultra HNW protocol verification. Documentation audits will examine random sampling of client files monthly rather than quarterly. Investment committee pre-approval is mandatory for alternative investments exceeding $10 million or representing more than 20% of client portfolio allocation.

VIOLATION CONSEQUENCES:
Non-compliance with these enhanced standards may result in immediate suspension of client management privileges and potential regulatory referral. The firm's regulatory capital requirements increase proportionally with Ultra HNW client base, making strict adherence essential for maintaining our broker-dealer registration.

Questions regarding implementation should be directed to the Compliance Department within 48 hours. Training sessions for all Private Wealth team members are scheduled for the first week of each quarter, with mandatory attendance required for license maintenance.

This memo supersedes all previous Ultra HNW documentation guidance and remains effective until further notice.
